text: Nicolas Bustamante profile: AI for knowledge workers at Microsoft. Previously CEO of Fintool, acquired by Microsoft, and CEO of Doctrine, acquired by Summit Partners. San Francisco; nicolasbustamante.com; 3,068 posts; 536 following; 2.2万 followers.

Pinned article announces Microsoft has acquired Fintool. Visible article preview ties the product to Warren Buffett-style work: reading filings, analyzing businesses, and building an investment process.

Nicolas asks which memory-generation approach people prefer. Claude real-time writes memories during the conversation based on user feedback. Codex async defers writes until a session is idle for at least six hours, then an asynchronous pipeline handles the write as a background job at the start of the next session. Quotes his Agent Memory Engineering article.

Memory security post: Nicolas says memory is a major attack vector because a memory entry that enters the system prompt can become authoritative instruction context. He notes Hermes has a Python script checking for environment-file extraction and prompt-injection phrases. Quotes his Agent Memory Engineering article.
Nicolas says the new Codex app is very good and the web browser integration is smooth. He says it bought four dining chairs for him and tags @thsottiaux and @romainhuet.
Nicolas says Codex is importing his Claude Code setup, while .codex/memories is still empty. This is a concrete memory-portability and setup-migration signal.
